channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Intune device compliance, including tenant-wide compliance policy settings and device compliance policies.

item.title: Monitor results of your device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device compliance dashboard to understand overall device compliance the per policy and per setting device compliance results.

item.title: Third-party device compliance partners support in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a third-party device compliance partner as a source of compliance data for devices you manage with Intune.

item.title: Create device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create device compliance policies for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manually configure Jamf Pro integration with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manually configure Jamf Pro integration with Intune when you can't use the Jamf Cloud Connector.

item.title: Use Conditional Access with Microsoft Intune compliance policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Combine Conditional Access with Intune compliance policies to define the requirements that users and devices must meet before gaining access to your organization's resources.

item.title: Use custom compliance settings for Linux and Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage custom compliance settings for Linux and Windows devices by using JSON files and discovery scripts in Intune.

item.title: Scenarios for using Conditional Access with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Conditional Access is commonly used with Intune compliance policy for devices and apps

item.title: Create discovery scripts for custom compliance policy in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create scripts for Linux or Windows devices to discover the settings you define as custom compliance settings for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use app-based Conditional Access policies with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about using app-based Conditional Access policies with Microsoft Intune for both enrolled and unenrolled devices.

item.title: Join your cloud-native endpoints to Microsoft Entra - Microsoft Intune
item.description: When moving to or using cloud-native endpoints, use Microsoft Entra joined endpoints. When your endpoints are joined to Microsoft Entra, you can use Windows Autopilot to provision or get devices ready for organization use. Learn more about the benefits to IT admins and end-users.

item.title: Send Email Notifications to Noncompliant Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create notification templates and send email alerts to users with noncompliant devices in Microsoft Intune. Configure actions for noncompliance.

item.title: macOS device compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for macOS devices that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Create a JSON file for custom compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create the JSON file that defines custom settings and values for use with device compliance policies in Intune.

item.title: View the Android device administrator compliance settings for Microsoft Intune compliance policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings that are available for Android device administrator devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Compliance for Windows Subsystem for Linux - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Evaluate WSL attributes on a host device for compliance.

item.title: Android (AOSP) compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for Android (AOSP) that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Linux device compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for Linux that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: What are cloud-native Windows endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about cloud-native endpoints and what they are. See a list of benefits, and the effect on end users and IT administrators. Cloud-native endpoints help with remote workers and hybrid workers, and use Microsoft Intune to manage devices.

item.title: Tutorial - Set up cloud-native Windows endpoints with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step-by-step tutorial to set up a cloud-native Windows endpoint - Microsoft Entra joined, Intune enrolled, secured, and deployed with Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Update your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To support hybrid and remote workers, convert or migrate your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints. This planning guide focuses on deploying apps and updates with Intune, moving from Group Policy Objects, and using Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Use on-premises services with cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: For cloud-native endpoints to access on-premises resources, such as file servers, printers, and web servers, use Windows integrated authentication (WIA) and Microsoft Entra Connect.

item.title: Common issues and resolutions with cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the known and resolutions when using cloud-native endpoints. Use user-based authentication; don't use machine authentication. Existing group policy objects might not apply.

item.title: Block Apps with No Modern Authentication on Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about applications and modern authentication (MSAL) using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage access to Exchange without device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to give employees access to their Microsoft 365 Exchange Online email without setting up a device management system.

item.title: Configure compliance policies with actions for noncompliance in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure your compliance policies with one or more actions for noncompliance to protect devices and your organization from unprotected devices. Actions can remotely lock devices, send email or notifications to device users, and more.

item.title: Set up device-based Conditional Access policies with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure a device-based Conditional Access policy that uses device compliance status from Intune device compliance policies.

item.title: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune to target policies based on device properties like OS version or manufacturer. Learn to create, update, and delete filters for managed devices and apps.

item.title: Set up enrollment for Android Enterprise fully managed devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment in Intune for devices using the Android Enterprise fully managed device management solution.

item.title: Assign device profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to assign device configuration profiles and policies to users and devices. Learn how to exclude groups from a profile assignment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Categorize devices into groups in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Categorize Intune-managed devices into groups for easier management in the admin center.

item.title: Tutorial - Walkthrough the Microsoft Intune Admin Center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you will tour Microsoft Intune in the Microsoft Intune admin center to better understand how to accomplish tasks.

item.title: Add groups to organize users and devices for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create Microsoft Entra groups to organize users and devices for use with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Assignment Filter Performance Tips for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Optimize Microsoft Intune performance with assignment filters. Learn to use virtual groups, reuse groups, and apply filters effectively. Improve policy deployment speed with incremental group changes, and use assignment filters to include and exclude.

item.title: Walkthrough-Create a settings catalog policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This tutorial or walkthrough steps through creating and comparing an on-premises Administrative Templates (ADMX) Group Policy and Microsoft Intune cloud-based settings catalog policy. It shows similar settings in on-premises and the Intune settings catalog to create and manage policies for Office, Windows, and Microsoft Edge on Windows 10/11 client devices.

item.title: Plan Education device grouping and targeting - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan how you'll group devices and users and target policies and applications.

item.title: macOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll macOS devices using device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Questions with policies and profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Common questions, answers, and scenarios with device policies and profiles in Microsoft Intune. Learn more about profile changes not applying to users or devices, how long it takes for new policies to deploy, which settings apply when there are conflicts, what happens when you delete or remove a profile, and more.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Update Windows BIOS using configuration MDM policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the BIOS configuration profile that can manage traditional BIOS settings in Microsoft Intune. You use an OEM tool to create a configuration file with the settings. You deploy an OEM Win32 app to the devices to read the configuration file and BIOS passwords. Then, you create a BIOS configuration policy in Intune to add the configuration file and assign the policy to your devices.

item.title: Change Review Agent Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Change Review Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, and how it works.

item.title: Android Open Source Project Supported Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Lists Android open source project devices (AOSP) supported devices

item.title: Configure Platform SSO for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure Platform SSO and deploy the configuration to your macOS devices. Platform SSO enables single sign-on (SSO) using Microsoft Entra ID with the Secure Enclave, smart card, or password authentication methods. You create a settings catalog policy to configure the settings. This article is a step-by-step guide to configure Platform SSO for macOS devices using Intune.

item.title: Prerequisites for the Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the software and network prerequisites for use of the Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune provides specific report types with focused views that contain consistent and timely data.

item.title: Deploy endpoint detection and response policy with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune endpoint security policies to configure and deploy Microsoft Defender for Endpoint EDR capabilities to Windows, macOS, and Linux devices.

item.title: Overview of Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the unified Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune, which supports SCEP, PKCS, imported PKCS, and certificate revocation.

item.title: Add Platform SSO policy to ADE Profile on macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add a settings catalog platform single sign-on (PSSO) policy to an Automated Device Enrollment (ADE) profile and configure it to run during Setup Assistant with modern authentication on macOS devices.

item.title: Set up local admin account creation and password management for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up macOS account configuration with LAPS through automatic device enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: Add an Android Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add a Android line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Apple device restriction settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, or create settings on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS devices to restrict features in Microsoft Intune. Create password requirements, control the locked screen, use built-in apps, add restricted or approved apps, handle bluetooth devices, connect to the cloud for backup and storage, enable kiosk mode, add domains, and control how users interact with the Safari web browser.

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure Multi Admin Approval to protect your tenant against the use of compromised administrative accounts in Intune.

item.title: Configure Universal Print Policy Using Settings Catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure a Universal Print policy using the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to automatically install printers on managed Windows devices.

item.title: Common tasks and features in the settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure common features. You can create a Universal Print policy, configure Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome web browsers, and use built in settings instead of plist files for macOS devices.

item.title: Settings you can manage with Intune Endpoint Protection profiles for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the available settings in Intune endpoint protection profiles for managed Windows devices.

item.title: Settings list for the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the settings in the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and each settings default value.

item.title: Manage antivirus settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies and use reports for devices you manage with endpoint security antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Platform SSO scenarios for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure common Platform SSO scenarios for macOS devices. You can enable Kerberos SSO to on-premises Active Directory and cloud-based Microsoft Entra ID, use Touch ID biometric policy with Secure Enclave authentication, and enable SSO on non-Microsoft apps. You can also configure end user experience settings.

item.title: Create Mobile Threat Defense compliance policies in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create an Intune device compliance policy that uses your MTD partner threat levels to determine if a mobile device can access company resources.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Macs into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Upgrade the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway server software - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand how Microsoft Tunnel Gateway upgrades to new versions of the tunnel software for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Zero Trust with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune contributes to a strong Zero Trust strategy and architecture by managing and securing endpoints that access organizational resources.

item.title: Encrypt Windows devices with BitLocker using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to manage BitLocker encryption on Windows devices, including silent encryption and Personal Data Encryption.

item.title: Unlicensed administrator access to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about unlicensed administrator access in Microsoft Intune, including default behavior for newer tenants and how to enable it for older tenants.

item.title: Understand Microsoft Intune Management Extension - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand Microsoft Intune management extension for Windows.

item.title: Plan for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the requirements and capabilities of Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Add an Unmanaged macOS PKG App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an unmanaged macOS PKG app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add a macOS DMG App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add a macOS DMG app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: How to Add macOS Line-of-Business Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add macOS line-of-business (LOB) apps to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an iOS/iPadOS Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an iOS/iPadOS line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Understanding Microsoft Intune Management Agent for macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Microsoft Intune management agent for macOS.

item.title: Custom per-app VPN profile for Android DA in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a custom profile for per-app VPN profiles on Android device administrator with the Pulse Secure or Citrix VPN connection types in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Query - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device query in Microsoft Intune to get on-demand device state, run Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries, and troubleshoot devices.

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run KQL queries, analyze inventory trends, and start querying your managed fleet now.

item.title: Microsoft Intune documentation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Explore guides, tutorials, and references for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Recovery Lock using the settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create settings using the settings catalog to configure Recovery Lock on macOS devices. Use Recovery Lock to help protect your macOS devices against unauthorized reinstallation and wiping. Using Microsoft Intune, you can configure Recovery Lock settings, and deploy these settings to macOS devices in your organization.

item.title: Configure Apple account service access - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Apple access management settings to control how Apple accounts are used on organization‑owned devices managed by Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Intune Data Storage and Processing Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Intune stores and processes personal data, including storage locations, data residency, and retention policies.

item.title: Privacy and personal data in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn what personal data is collected and processed in Intune.

item.title: Govern and administer - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure role-based access control, scope tags, audit logs, and privacy settings to govern and administer Microsoft Intune at scale.

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Enroll devices using a device enrollment manager account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device enrollment manager account to enroll devices in Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Wipe - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to wipe, or factory reset, devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect your Intune account to your Managed Google Play account.

item.title: Enable Windows diagnostic data and license verification - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enable Intune tenant settings to use Windows diagnostic data and verify Windows licenses required for dependent features.

item.title: Add corporate identifiers to Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add corporate identifiers (enrollment method, IMEI, and serial numbers) to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Restrict USB Devices Using Settings Catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to restrict USB devices, including thumb drives, flash drives, USB cameras, and more. Use other settings to allow specific USB devices on Windows 10/11 devices.

item.title: Endpoint analytics overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics provides actionable insights to optimize device performance, improve user experience, and enable proactive IT troubleshooting.

item.title: Set up Intune enrollment for Android Enterprise dedicated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure enrollment in Microsoft Intune for Android Enterprise dedicated devices.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS direct enrollment - Apple Configurator-Setup Assistant - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Apple Configurator to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices with Setup Assistant.

item.title: Bulk enrollment for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a bulk enrollment package for Microsoft Intune

item.title: Device Action: Retire - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to retire device using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Locate Device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Locate lost or stolen devices by using the locate device feature in Microsoft Intune. Get details on security and privacy information when using the locate device action.

item.title: Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, tvOS, visionOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available device actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Collect Diagnostics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to collect diagnostics with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up enrollment time grouping - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview and setup of the enrollment time grouping feature in Microsoft Intune.
